content.question: What is the actual specialist-teacher-to-SNI-pupil ratio by district and county, and does this ratio differ significantly between high-prevalence regions (e.g., Békés) and low-recorded-prevalence regions (e.g., Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén), and is any disparity attributable to workforce supply or to assessment-access differences?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a detailed capacity audit mapping specialist staffing, traveling-network coverage, Expert Committee throughput, and assessment wait times by county; cross-tabulate against recorded SEN prevalence and against socioeconomic/disadvantage indicators to distinguish service-supply constraints from diagnostic-access or identification-quality differences.
